name: long-context-summarization
whenToUse: Condense long documents, transcripts, or many files into a faithful, citable summary without losing decision-critical detail.
workflow: Segment the source into coherent chunks with stable ids.
Summarize each chunk independently, preserving claims + their source span.
Merge chunk summaries hierarchically; deduplicate and resolve contradictions.
Produce a working-memory summary plus a detail index (claim -> source chunk).
Verify coverage: every required section/entity from the source appears.
verification: No claim in the summary is absent from the source (no hallucinated detail).
Every decision-critical entity from the source is represented.
Summary stays within the token budget.
commonFailures: Dropping decision-critical detail to hit a length target.
Hallucinating connective claims not in the source.
Losing the source mapping so claims can't be traced.
Letting later chunks overwrite earlier facts.

examples.input: summarize a 40-page design doc, focus on risks
examples.output: 12-bullet risk summary + claimIndex mapping each risk to its section id; coverageGaps: none.
